name: fcn

members.name: FCNConfig
members.signature: FCNConfig(num_classes: int = 21, in_channels: int = 3, backbone: str = 'resnet50', variant: str = 'fcn32s', classifier_hidden_channels: int = 512, aux_hidden_channels: int = 256, dropout: float = 0.1)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 21, in_channels: int = 3, backbone: str = 'resnet50', variant: str = 'fcn32s', classifier_hidden_channels: int = 512, aux_hidden_channels: int = 256, dropout: float = 0.1)

members.extended:
FCN adapts a classification CNN backbone (ResNet) into a dense
predictor by replacing fully-connected layers with convolutions and
adding upsampling to restore spatial resolution.

parameters.name: classifier_hidden_channels
parameters.description: Hidden channels in the FCN segmentation head.

parameters.name: aux_hidden_channels
parameters.description: Hidden channels in the auxiliary head.

notes:
ResNet backbone with dilated convolutions (layer3 dilation=2,
layer4 dilation=4) → FCN head (3×3 conv + BN + ReLU + 1×1 conv)
→ bilinear upsample to input resolution.

An auxiliary head on layer3 output provides additional supervision
during training.

members.name: FCNForSemanticSegmentation

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, targets: Tensor | None = None)

parameters.name: targets
parameters.description: Optional (B, H, W) integer ground-truth masks.

members.summary: Fully Convolutional Network for semantic segmentation (Long et al., CVPR 2015).
members.extended:
The original deep-learning semantic segmentation architecture: a
classification backbone is repurposed as a *fully convolutional*
network by replacing fully-connected layers with 1x1 convolutions
and adding a final upsampling stage to recover input resolution.
In Lucid's implementation the backbone is a dilated ResNet
(`layer3` dilation = 2, `layer4` dilation = 4) so the deepest
feature map stays at stride 8 instead of stride 32, giving denser
spatial sampling without sacrificing the receptive field.

A primary FCN head produces `num_classes` logits from the C5
feature, and an auxiliary head on C4 stabilises optimisation
(used at training time only).  Bilinear interpolation lifts both
heads to the input resolution.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen architecture spec.  Use `fcn_resnet50` /
`fcn_resnet101` for the standard variants.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.fcn import fcn_resnet50
>>> model = fcn_resnet50()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 512, 512)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape    # (B, num_classes, H, W)
(1, 21, 512, 512)
notes:
See Long et al., "Fully Convolutional Networks for Semantic
Segmentation", CVPR 2015 ([arXiv:1411.4038](https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.4038)).  The bilinear
upsampling at the end is equivalent to a learnable transpose
convolution initialised to bilinear weights — but kept fixed in
this modernised dilated-ResNet variant.  Training loss is



$$
\mathcal{L} = \mathcal{L}_\mathrm{CE}(\mathrm{main}, y)
            + 0.4\, \mathcal{L}_\mathrm{CE}(\mathrm{aux}, y),
$$

with categorical cross-entropy summed over all pixels.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: backbone
attributes.description:
Dilated ResNet trunk producing C4 (stride 8, used by aux head)
and C5 (stride 8, used by main head) — both at the same
resolution thanks to atrous convolution in layer4.

attributes.name: classifier
attributes.description:
3x3 conv -> BatchNorm -> ReLU -> Dropout -> 1x1 conv producing
`num_classes` channels from the C5 feature.

attributes.name: aux_classifier
attributes.description:
Auxiliary head with the same structure applied to C4; its loss
is weighted by 0.4 and added to the main loss only during
training.

members.name: fcn_resnet50
members.signature: fcn_resnet50(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: FCNResNet50Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: FCN with ResNet-50 backbone (Long et al., CVPR 2015).
members.extended:
Builds an `FCNForSemanticSegmentation` with a dilated
ResNet-50 backbone (`layer3` dilation = 2, `layer4` dilation = 4),
21 output classes (Pascal VOC default), a 512-channel main FCN head,
and a 256-channel auxiliary head.  The dilation trick keeps the
final feature map at stride 8 instead of stride 32, retaining 16x
more spatial sampling for dense prediction.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` →
the `DEFAULT` tag (`FCNResNet50Weights.COCO_WITH_VOC_LABELS_V1`);
a tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over `pretrained`.

returns.description:
Segmentation model with the FCN-R50 configuration applied (or
with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.fcn import fcn_resnet50
>>> model = fcn_resnet50()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 512, 512)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 21, 512, 512)
notes:
See Long et al., "Fully Convolutional Networks for Semantic
Segmentation", CVPR 2015 ([arXiv:1411.4038](https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.4038)).  The dilated variant
follows the DeepLab v1 trick (Chen et al., 2015) of replacing
stride-2 downsampling in layer3 / layer4 with atrous convolution.

members.name: fcn_resnet101
members.signature: fcn_resnet101(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: FCNResNet101Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: FCN with ResNet-101 backbone (Long et al., CVPR 2015).
members.extended:
Builds an `FCNForSemanticSegmentation` with the same head
configuration as `fcn_resnet50` but a deeper dilated ResNet-101
backbone (23 blocks in layer3 vs 6 for ResNet-50).  Typically gains
1-2 mIoU points on Pascal VOC at the cost of ~70% more FLOPs.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` →
the `DEFAULT` tag (`FCNResNet101Weights.COCO_WITH_VOC_LABELS_V1`);
a tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over `pretrained`.

returns.description:
Segmentation model with the FCN-R101 configuration applied (or
with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.fcn import fcn_resnet101
>>> model = fcn_resnet101()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 512, 512)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 21, 512, 512)

members.name: FCNResNet50Weights
FCNResNet101Weights

summary: FCN — Fully Convolutional Network (Long et al., CVPR 2015).
extended: Paper: "Fully Convolutional Networks for Semantic Segmentation"
citation: Long, Jonathan, et al. "Fully Convolutional Networks for Semantic Segmentation." Proceedings of the IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition, 2015, pp. 3431–3440.
theory:
The Fully Convolutional Network is the first deep model trained
end-to-end, pixels-to-pixels, for **semantic segmentation**.  Its
core idea is to **convert a classification CNN into a dense
predictor** by reinterpreting its fully-connected layers as
$1 \times 1$ convolutions and learning to up-sample the
coarse spatial output back to input resolution.

A classifier such as VGG-16 ends with FC layers that collapse the
feature map to a single $1 \times 1 \times K$ vector.  By
rewriting each FC as a convolution with kernel equal to the input
feature size, the network becomes input-agnostic and produces a
*heatmap* $\mathbb{R}^{K \times H/s \times W/s}$ at the
backbone's coarse stride $s$ (32 for VGG-16, 32 for most
ResNets).

To recover full resolution the paper introduces **skip
architectures** — FCN-32s / FCN-16s / FCN-8s — that combine the
coarse final layer prediction with the higher-resolution
intermediate pool-4 and pool-3 predictions through learned
deconvolutions:



$$
\hat{y} = \mathrm{upsample}_{2}\!\bigl(
    \mathrm{upsample}_{2}(p_5) + p_4
\bigr) + p_3, \quad
\text{(FCN-8s sketch)},
$$

where $p_\ell$ is the per-class score map from level
$\ell$.  This multi-resolution fusion gives the model
*coarse* semantic information and *fine* spatial detail without
needing extra encoder–decoder parameters.

Training uses a per-pixel softmax cross-entropy loss summed over
all output positions; the entire model is jointly fine-tuned
from ImageNet-pretrained classification weights.  FCN
demonstrated for the first time that segmentation could be a
"natural" output mode of a CNN, paving the way for U-Net,
DeepLab, and every subsequent dense-prediction architecture.
